reasoning: Peer set (Denzi helmets) uses a consistently muted, cool-to-neutral metallic palette: silvers, dark grays, teal, and subdued bronze/gold. The candidate key_gold_v02 has a gold shaft that is arguably within range, but the ornate top decoration introduces vivid rainbow/multi-hue pixels (cyan, hot pink, bright yellow, green) that are entirely absent from the peer palette — this is the single biggest breach. Color temperature and saturation for that element spike well above any peer. Charm level is also a mismatch: the helmets read as utilitarian/stern equipment sprites; the rainbow gem makes the key feel distinctly magical/cute, a different tonal register. Rendering style (pixel art, 1-2px dark outlines) and shadow direction are consistent with peers, and detail density is comparable. Outline weight and highlight intensity look roughly aligned. Proportion difference (elongated key vs square helmets) is not penalized per item-type rules. Net: 2 hard failures (palette overlap, charm level) and 1 soft failure (saturation/temperature) against 7 acceptable or passing dimensions — lands in marginal territory.
